failure_surface: Agent implemented Python/openpyxl solution when instruction explicitly requested VBA code generation. Additionally, agent used example range (A1:M1) instead of actual data range (L26:SHA26) specified in instruction.
root_cause: Task type misinterpretation combined with failure to distinguish between illustrative example ranges and actual working ranges in multi-part instructions.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing code, verify the required output format (VBA vs Python vs Excel formula) and confirm which ranges represent actual data versus examples before selecting target cells.
evidence: Instruction explicitly states: 'How can I create a VBA code' - but solution implements Python with openpyxl
Instruction specifies actual data range: 'actual worksheet data range is L26 to SHA26' - but agent processed A1:M1 only
test_case_results shows [0] indicating complete evaluation failure
Solution writes Python code to spreadsheet file instead of generating VBA macro code as requested

memory_items.title: Match Tool to Requested Output Type
memory_items.lesson: When an instruction requests VBA/macros/Excel formulas, do not substitute with external programming languages like Python. Verify the required deliverable type before implementation.
memory_items.generalization: Identify and match the coding paradigm specified in instructions (VBA, Excel formulas, Python, JavaScript) before generating any code blocks.

memory_items.title: Distinguish Example Ranges from Actual Data Ranges
memory_items.lesson: Instructions may include example ranges for clarification purposes while specifying different actual working ranges. Always prioritize the explicitly stated 'actual' or 'real' data range over illustrative examples.
memory_items.generalization: When multiple ranges appear in an instruction, look for qualifiers like 'actual', 'real', or 'example' to determine which range represents the target data.

memory_items.title: Validate Against Original Deliverable Requirements
memory_items.lesson: After completing a task, verify that the output matches the requested format and content type, not just technical correctness of calculations.
memory_items.generalization: Confirm the output artifact type (file format, code language, location) aligns with instruction requirements before marking completion.

edits.content:
## Task-Specific Output Validation

### Output Format Compliance
- Match the requested delivery format (VBA macros, Excel formulas, Python scripts) without substitution.
- If an instruction specifies a programming environment or code type, do not replace it with alternatives.

### Range Identification Best Practices
- Distinguish between illustrative example ranges and actual working data ranges.
- Look for explicit qualifiers ('actual range', 'example', 'reference') to determine target scope.
- Verify range specifications match the described worksheet layout before implementation.

### Cross-Verification Checklist
- Confirm output file exists at expected location with correct naming conventions.
- Ensure generated content matches requested type (code snippet vs modified spreadsheet vs calculation result).
- Validate that all specified ranges from instructions are addressed in the solution.
